title: Render, state mutation, and I/O tangled in App::handle_slash_command
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Dimension: Architectural Alignment (Dependency Inversion)
Location: src/tui/app.rs handle_slash_command branches for /diff (1094), /init (1204), /doctor (1138), /review (1169), /files (1054), /export (883), /load (702)
Violation: Slash commands directly invoke std::process::Command (git, bash, openclaudia subprocess), std::fs::{read_dir, read_to_string, write}, std::fs::canonicalize, and config::load_config from *inside the TUI synchronous event loop*. These block the rendering thread — typing lags visibly while git diff runs. Moreover the domain (conversation logic) is shot through with infrastructure calls (shell, filesystem, config). The TUI UI layer should never directly import std::process.
Mandated Refactor: Define trait SystemGateway { fn git_diff(&self) -> Result<String>; fn git_diff_head(&self) -> Result<String>; fn list_dir(&self, path: &str) -> Result<Vec<Entry>>; fn run_shell(&self, cmd: &str) -> Result<ShellOutput>; fn export_markdown(&self, session: &TuiSession) -> Result<PathBuf>; } with a SyncSystemGateway impl. Dispatch long-running gateway calls via handle.spawn() so the TUI never blocks. Unit-test by providing a MockSystemGateway.
